Off Calendar Aug-06-2020 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances; Lawrence Siracusa appeared by zoom for Scott Hodges.
Brian Hom (Beneficiary) appeared by zoom in Propria Persona.

Mr. Siracusa stated that his Client is requesting fees pursuant to provision 9.4.8 in the trust, that the Trustee sold real property and is waiving fees for the extraordinary portion that he did, that there was significant litigation, that other Professional Fiduciaries declined to serve due to the complexity of this trust and that his Client's appointment was good for the Beneficiaries. Mr. Siracusa also stated that the services provided by the Trustee significantly benefited the trust, that the Trustee would not have taken this on if the fees were limited to an hourly rate, that they had mediation, that they were able to reach a settlement agreement and that there is no reason to continue Court supervision. In addition, Mr. Siracusa stated that his vision is that another petition will not be filed, that they have to figure out how to proceed with distribution and whether to do it pro rata, and that he is confident that they will not have to come back for final distribution. The First Account and Report of Successor Trustee, etc. is under submission.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: C. Cabasares. Not reported.

Petition For Instructions Regarding Litigation And Petition To Set Bond
Off Calendar Feb-06-2019 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Lawrence Siracusa for Scott Hodges (Trustee).
Richard Bryan for Brian Hom and Anna Hom.
Allen Yee for Glenn Hom (Beneficiary).

Mr. Siracusa stated that it is his understanding that an elder abuse claim during the life of a Conservatee can only be maintained by the Conservator, that his Client believes he has a fiduciary duty to proceed with the action, that the discovery ruling has significantly strengthened this case against the Defendant and that even if they don't proceed, the trust should be paying the Conservatee. Mr. Siracusa also stated that he structured the petition in a way for the Court to get a sense of what it will take to go through trial and that he feels they need the Court's instruction because one of the Defendants is a residuary Beneficiary of the trust. Mr. Yee stated that he thinks discovery is very significant in terms of moving forward in this case and that he supports the petition. Mr. Bryan stated that the funds at issue here are not part of the trust, that the accounts were maintained by the Senior Hom's (Grandparents) and became funds of the conservatorship when Ms. Hom became conserved, raised the issue the Trustee favoring one beneficiary and the issue of conflict, and stated that he feels the Trustee's participation should be limited. The Petition for Instructions Regarding Litigation and Petition to Set Bond is under submission. The Petition to Direct Transfer of Property to Trustee, etc. on calendar for February 13, 2019 is continued to March 13, 2019 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: John K. Stewart. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Chan. Not reported.

Notice Of Motion To Quash Or Alternatively Motion For Protective Order And Supporting Memorandum
Off Calendar Jan-22-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Daniel Conrad for Gene Marchi and Andrea Leung (Conservators).
Alan Lee for Glenn Hom.
Richard Brian for Brian Hom and Anna Hom.

Matter called. The Court and counsel confer regarding "cash deposits" and "check/wire transfer" language.

At the conclusion of discussions, the Court adopted the tentative ruling as follows: Brian and Anna Hom's Motion to Quash is denied in part and granted in part. The Motion to Quash is not procedurally defective for lack of a separate statement given CRC 3.1345(b). If the subpoenaed banks possess responsive records which indicate on the face of such records that deposits were in the form of cash, the subpoenaed records reflecting cash deposits of $300 or more, made between June 2010 and April 2014, into the specified accounts shall be produced. To the extent bank records do not state whether deposits were in the form of cash, they shall not be produced. The banks may redact all but the last four digits of any account numbers and the names of any non-party joint account holders; and, in any event, such information shall be redacted from the records and not be communicated to anyone else by the attorney receiving any such records. Any responsive records produced are subject to the Protective Order the Court adopted on March 15, 2018, and any dissemination beyond attorneys is subject to the procedures provided in that order. All responses and production of information and records must be made within 20 days of this order. No sanctions are awarded. Mr. Yee is to submit an order that repeats verbatim the tentative ruling.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda. Bailiff: Deputy Chan. Matter Not Reported.

MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Daniel Conrad for Gene Marchi (trustee) and Andrea Leung (conservator). Richard Bryan for Brian Hom and Anna Hom. Alan Yee for Glen Hom.

Mr. Yee stated that there was a slight error in the order #4 and this was the reason for today's motion. Mr. Bryan argued that he believed that he was in compliance with providing the information in his responses.

After hearing oral argument and reviewing the court filed documents, the Court adopted the tentative ruling as follows: Petitioner Glenn Hom's Motion to Compel is granted in part and denied in part. The Court sees no reason to reconsider the March 15, 2018 Order ("the prior Order") or to go beyond the statutory verification protections to the extent Respondents Brian and Anna Hom ("Respondents") have verified responses that they have no responsive information. Nor is it appropriate for the Court to reiterate what it has already ordered. On the other hand, the Court is not satisfied that Respondents have demonstrated diligence in obtaining all account records within their custody or control needed to respond completely as required by the prior Order. Within 30 days of the date of this order, Respondents are ordered to (1) obtain copies of the "missing" records for Anna Hom's account(s) (and for Brian Hom's accounts if there are any "missing" records as to his accounts) and review those records for responsive information and documents under the parameters set forth in the prior Order, and supplement their responses accordingly; (2) disclose the last four digits of any account originally held by Lester Hom, to which Brian and/or Anna became a successor in interest ("Lester Accounts"), regardless of whether Respondents have actual statements for those accounts currently in their possession; (3) make a diligent attempt to obtain records for any and all Lester Accounts, and supplement their responses accordingly; and (4) produce the deposit information that corresponds to the checks identified in Special Interrogatories 10 and 12 (regarding checks made out to Brian Hom in the amounts of $10,000 and $26,000). All other relief sought in Petitioner's motion is denied. No sanctions are awarded. (order for the same signed in open court).

Judge Peter J. Busch, Clerk: N. Wong, Bailiff: Deputy Wu. Not Reported.

PETITION FOR INSTRUCTIONS
Off Calendar Apr-16-2015 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Lawrence Siracusa for (petitioner) Gene Marchi. Court Appointed counsel Charles Jonas for (Conservatee) Marie Hom. Francesca Callejo for (objectors & beneficiaries) Brian & Anna Hom. Brent Kato for (conservator) Andrea Leung.

The court stated that we are here with respect to setting rent for a real property unit that had been occupied by the conservatee and that objections had been filed. Mr. Siracusa clarified that they are not asking to set the rent but for instructions on how to proceed with the process, stated that Brian & Anna Hom have lived rent free for many years, that Mrs. Hom's expenses exceed her income, that there has been no indication that Brian & Anna are unable to pay rent, that there seems a sense of entitlement and that it is unfair to Mrs. Hom and the residual beneficiaries. Ms. Callejo stated that she understands the trustee needs to protect himself, that Brian is now a 65% residual beneficiary, that there is concern regarding the cost of multiple attorneys and fiduciaries to the trust. Mr. Jonas reported that the unit is very old, that the rent would be low, that there would still need to be substantial use of the principal for Marie's care, that she seems content in the lower unit, is not sure if she would ever want to move back to the 3rd floor, and is not sure of her capacity or lack thereof. Mr. Kato agreed that her capacity is unclear and that when having conversations regarding the estate she says it should go to the children. Ms. Callejo stated that renting the upper unit is problematic, if the rent is too high Brian & Anna may move, that the upper unit is Marie's former home and she is able to visit Brian & Anna there. Mr. Siracusa stated again that if Brian & Anna cannot afford the rent, something could be worked out. Mr. Kato stated that the conservator would agree that Marie would like to be close to Brian and that her move downstairs has facilitated the visitation from other family members. The court stated that this presents difficult questions, that there is family history involved, but that the circumstances have changed, the 3rd floor occupants are no longer living with the conservatee, that the trustee needs to do what is proper regarding the trust property and that the objectors do not have an invested right in living rent free. The Court granted the petition for instructions (subject to review of the order). The trustee is to proceed with the determination of reasonable rent.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Allen. (Not Reported)
